selected_headline.main_headline: The 1-Swing Method 150,000 Beginners Used to Hit Flush Shots Without Changing Setup Club-to-Club
selected_headline.subheadline: One stance, one motion, one practice path that helps new golfers build consistent contact before bad habits set in.

headline_architecture.curiosity_gap: How one setup and one motion can replace club-to-club adjustments while still producing consistent contact for beginners.
headline_architecture.big_idea_expression: Beginners improve faster when they use one repeatable setup and one swing for every club instead of managing multiple club-specific swings.

lead_handoff.headline_callback_opportunity: Open the lead by exposing why club-to-club setup changes overload beginners, then introduce the one-swing method as the relief point.

alternatives.headline: A Top-7 Coach's One-Swing Beginner System: Solid Contact Now, Break 100 in 1-2 Months
alternatives.subheadline: Learn the same Universal Setup and One Swing Motion Martin Chuck used to help new golfers improve on a shorter timeline.
alternatives.rationale: High authority and outcome specificity, slightly less curiosity than the selected winner.

alternatives.headline: Beginners: Same Setup, Same Swing, Every Club - Start Making Solid Contact in 15 Minutes
alternatives.subheadline: Stop switching ball position and swing thoughts on every shot; use one repeatable motion from driver through wedge.
alternatives.rationale: Very clear and specific; broad-proof element is weaker than the selected winner.

vault_inspiration.pattern_borrowed: Mechanism + quantified social proof + immediate beginner outcome
vault_inspiration.differentiation: Uses golf-beginner language from the provided VSL and ties directly to one-swing across clubs.
